steps.title: Identify what each noun is doing
steps.content: "Hands" is the object of "place" and "antennas" is the object of the preposition "between." Neither noun owns anything, so neither needs an apostrophe; both should be simple plurals.

steps.title: Apply the plural-vs-possessive rule
steps.content: Plural nouns that show no ownership take just an -s ending, with no apostrophe. The only option that writes both nouns as plain plurals is "hands... antennas."

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘hand's between the two antenna's,’: uses singular possessive "hand’s" (you place both hands, not one) and incorrectly puts an apostrophe on the plural "antennas." ✗
  • Choice B — ‘hands between the two antennas,’: correct plural form for both nouns, no false possessives. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘hands' between the two antennas',’: adds possessive apostrophes to both plural nouns, but nothing is being owned. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘hands' between the two antennas,’: still treats "hands’" as possessive when it is just the object of "place." ✗
That confirms B is correct.
